title: Installing podman crashes existing kube cluster running on CRIO
body:
### Issue Description

Installing podman using the installation instructions on a node:
```
sudo apt-get -y install podman
```

I have an existing kubernetes cluster running using the same node as master node.
Installing podman seems to completely  cripple my existing kubernetes cluster.
Any containers started or restarted after the podman installation fail with `Error: CreateContainerError: unknown version specified`

Note that I did not change the container runtime for my cluster, nor any configs. I did not do anything except install podman using apt-get.
I have no idea why this should cripple my running cluster.

`kubectl get events` also sometimes shoes `rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: desc = "transport: Error while dialing: dial unix /var/run/crio/crio.sock: connect: no such file or directory"`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have a healthy kubernetes cluster running on CRI-O
2. Install podman using steps in the docs
3. New container created and restarts fail in existing kubernetes cluster.


### Describe the results you received

Pods fail to start with `Error: CreateContainerError: unknown version specified`

### Describe the results you expected

Simply installing podman should not affect my running cluster, until I explicitly switch over to podman as the container runtime.


### podman info output

```yaml
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.23.1
cgroupControllers:
- memory
- pids
cgroupManager: systemd
cgroupVersion: v2
conmon:
  package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
  path: /usr/bin/conmon
  version: 'conmon version 2.0.25, commit: unknown'
cpus: x
distribution:
  codename: jammy
  distribution: ubuntu
  version: "22.04"
eventLogger: journald
...
kernel: 6.8.0-45-generic
ociRuntime:
  name: crun
  package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
  path: /ust/bin/crun
  version:
    crun version 0.17
...
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Please help

comments.body:
Upstream only supports the latest version and you installing some really old version on ubuntu 2204.

You also did not specify how crio was installed but regardless `unknown version specified` is a crun error that happened when using a newer podman/crio which generated a spec which a version not supported by that old crun.

You would have to update crun or use an older crio

Thanks for your response!

After installing podman, my current crun version is 0.17 from `crun --version`
Is this the right, latest crun version to be used?

I installed both cri-o and podman as per the latest docs:

https://cri-o.io (tldr: Add crio repo and `apt-get install cri-o`)
https://podman.io/docs/installation#linux-distributions (tldr: `sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get -y install podman`)

Is there something wrong with my process? Why did a really old version get installed by following the latest live installation docs?

Thanks again!

podman comes from the offical ubuntu repo and LTS distros like ubuntu basically never update most package versions (except for security fixes). That means you get 3+ year old version on 2204.

crio seems to have a third party repos so they don't come from ubuntu and mixing different repos and external packages is generally always something that can cause problems on any distro. 

There are plenty of discussions on this repo about getting newer podman versions but this is not something we upstream can help with https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17362
